ABCD_SubMod_StatAnalysis_020326

Felicia Hardi

2026-02-03

Load packages

library(broom)
library(cluster)
library(dplyr)
library(emmeans)
library(factoextra)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(Hmisc)
library(interactions)
library(kableExtra)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
library(magrittr)
library(NbClust)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(table1)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

Test if bpm-brain associations differ by groups, with age x sex

brains <- c("dmn","sal","fpn","dmn_sal","dmn_fpn","sal_fpn")

families <- list(
  brain_clust         = ":clust",
  brain_clust_age     = ":clust:age_c",
  brain_clust_sex     = ":clust:sex",
  brain_clust_age_sex = ":clust:age_c:sex"
)

outcomes <- c("bpm_int", "bpm_ext")

mods <- lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  m <- lapply(brains, \(b)
    lmer(
      as.formula(paste0(
        y, " ~ ", b, " * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + ",
        "(1|src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1|site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_z"
      )),
      data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age_c)),
      na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights
    )
  )
  names(m) <- brains
  m
})
## Warning: Using one column matrices in `filter()` was deprecated in dplyr 1.1.0.
## ℹ Please use one dimensional logical vectors instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
names(mods) <- outcomes

anv <- lapply(mods, \(mm) lapply(mm, anova))

p_all <- bind_rows(lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  bind_rows(lapply(names(families), \(fam) {
    suf <- families[[fam]]
    pvec <- sapply(brains, \(b) anv[[y]][[b]][paste0(b, suf), "Pr(>F)"])
    tibble(outcome = y, family = fam, brain = brains, p = pvec, p_fdr = p.adjust(pvec, "fdr"))
  }))
})) 

p_all %>% filter(p_fdr < .05) %>% arrange(outcome, brain)
## # A tibble: 13 × 5
##    outcome family              brain            p     p_fdr
##    <chr>   <chr>               <chr>        <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1 bpm_ext brain_clust_age     dmn_fpn 0.0146     0.0370   
##  2 bpm_ext brain_clust_age_sex dmn_fpn 0.000857   0.00171  
##  3 bpm_ext brain_clust_age     fpn     0.000147   0.000883 
##  4 bpm_ext brain_clust_age_sex fpn     0.00000998 0.0000599
##  5 bpm_ext brain_clust_age_sex sal     0.0152     0.0228   
##  6 bpm_ext brain_clust_age     sal_fpn 0.0185     0.0370   
##  7 bpm_ext brain_clust_sex     sal_fpn 0.00132    0.00790  
##  8 bpm_ext brain_clust_age_sex sal_fpn 0.000335   0.00100  
##  9 bpm_int brain_clust_age     dmn     0.00182    0.00547  
## 10 bpm_int brain_clust_sex     dmn     0.00497    0.0149   
## 11 bpm_int brain_clust         sal_fpn 0.0000113  0.0000675
## 12 bpm_int brain_clust_age     sal_fpn 0.000506   0.00303  
## 13 bpm_int brain_clust_sex     sal_fpn 0.00458    0.0149
anv <- lapply(mods, \(mm) lapply(mm, anova))

p_all <- bind_rows(lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  bind_rows(lapply(brains, \(b) {
    a <- as.data.frame(anv[[y]][[b]])
    tibble(
      outcome = y,
      brain   = b,
      term    = rownames(a),
      p       = a[["Pr(>F)"]],
      term_std = vapply(strsplit(rownames(a), ":"), \(x) {
        paste(replace(x, x == b, "brain"), collapse = ":")
      }, character(1))
    )
  }))
})) %>%
  group_by(outcome, term_std) %>%
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()

p_all %>% arrange(outcome) %>% 
  filter(brain=="sal_fpn" & outcome=="bpm_int") %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3)))
## # A tibble: 19 × 6
##    outcome brain   term                        p term_std              p_fdr
##    <chr>   <chr>   <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                 <dbl>
##  1 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn                 0.047 brain                 0.07 
##  2 bpm_int sal_fpn clust                   0     clust                 0    
##  3 bpm_int sal_fpn age_c                   0.009 age_c                 0.026
##  4 bpm_int sal_fpn sex                     0     sex                   0    
##  5 bpm_int sal_fpn changeage               0.684 changeage             0.71 
##  6 bpm_int sal_fpn rsfmri_meanmotion       0.005 rsfmri_meanmotion     0.016
##  7 bpm_int sal_fpn mri_info_manufacturer   0.198 mri_info_manufacturer 0.278
##  8 bpm_int sal_fpn pub_z                   0     pub_z                 0.001
##  9 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:clust           0     brain:clust           0    
## 10 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:age_c           0.351 brain:age_c           0.421
## 11 bpm_int sal_fpn clust:age_c             0.004 clust:age_c           0.008
## 12 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:sex             0.104 brain:sex             0.264
## 13 bpm_int sal_fpn clust:sex               0.201 clust:sex             0.402
## 14 bpm_int sal_fpn age_c:sex               0     age_c:sex             0    
## 15 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:clust:age_c     0.001 brain:clust:age_c     0.003
## 16 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:clust:sex       0.005 brain:clust:sex       0.015
## 17 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:age_c:sex       0.536 brain:age_c:sex       0.765
## 18 bpm_int sal_fpn clust:age_c:sex         0.001 clust:age_c:sex       0.004
## 19 bpm_int sal_fpn sal_fpn:clust:age_c:sex 0.195 brain:clust:age_c:sex 0.39

Probe significant models

# main finding
mod_sal_fpn_int <- lmer(
    bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)
anova(mod_sal_fpn_int)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                         Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                  2.419   2.419     1 11027.0  3.9598 0.0466232 *  
## clust                   66.038  33.019     2  4989.7 54.0546 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_c                    4.211   4.211     1  9456.0  6.8945 0.0086601 ** 
## sex                      9.093   9.093     1  5977.5 14.8856 0.0001154 ***
## changeage                0.101   0.101     1  2924.5  0.1659 0.6838219    
## pub_z                    7.463   7.463     1  5985.2 12.2168 0.0004770 ***
## rsfmri_meanmotion        4.718   4.718     1 10751.5  7.7237 0.0054596 ** 
## mri_info_manufacturer    2.352   1.176     2     9.5  1.9250 0.1984950    
## sal_fpn:clust           13.936   6.968     2 11060.6 11.4068 1.125e-05 ***
## sal_fpn:age_c            0.531   0.531     1  9851.5  0.8698 0.3510230    
## clust:age_c              6.767   3.384     2  9356.0  5.5392 0.0039426 ** 
## sal_fpn:sex              1.612   1.612     1 11048.2  2.6392 0.1042858    
## clust:sex                1.959   0.980     2  5884.8  1.6038 0.2012229    
## age_c:sex               55.682  55.682     1  9336.0 91.1548 < 2.2e-16 ***
## sal_fpn:clust:age_c      9.279   4.640     2  9850.2  7.5952 0.0005058 ***
## sal_fpn:clust:sex        6.585   3.292     2 11052.2  5.3897 0.0045752 ** 
## sal_fpn:age_c:sex        0.234   0.234     1  9859.9  0.3828 0.5361261    
## clust:age_c:sex          8.953   4.477     2  9355.1  7.3285 0.0006603 ***
## sal_fpn:clust:age_c:sex  1.999   1.000     2  9852.2  1.6363 0.1947579    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
test(emtrends(mod_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", at = list(age_c = 0)), adjust = "fdr") # 2-way slope difference
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.865 0.569 Inf   1.521  0.1283
##  2             4.897 1.130 Inf   4.338 <0.0001
##  3            -2.470 1.060 Inf  -2.340  0.0289
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex, mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(mod_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", at = list(age_c = 0)), adjust = "fdr") # 2-way slope difference
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -4.03 1.26 Inf  -3.200  0.0021
##  clust1 - clust3     3.34 1.20 Inf   2.787  0.0053
##  clust2 - clust3     7.37 1.54 Inf   4.775 <0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex, mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(mod_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "sal_fpn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.505 1.110 Inf   1.353  0.5286
##  2            -0.158 2.130 Inf  -0.074  0.9407
##  3            -1.177 1.980 Inf  -0.594  0.8286
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.550 1.020 Inf   0.542  0.8822
##  2             3.317 1.930 Inf   1.717  0.2580
##  3            -0.149 1.940 Inf  -0.077  0.9390
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.646 0.803 Inf   2.049  0.1214
##  2             1.542 1.590 Inf   0.970  0.3321
##  3            -2.564 1.520 Inf  -1.683  0.1384
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.084 0.800 Inf   0.105  0.9164
##  2             8.252 1.600 Inf   5.158 <0.0001
##  3            -2.377 1.460 Inf  -1.630  0.1548
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.787 1.030 Inf   1.731  0.1186
##  2             3.242 2.080 Inf   1.561  0.1186
##  3            -3.950 2.030 Inf  -1.945  0.1186
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1            -0.382 1.130 Inf  -0.338  0.7356
##  2            13.187 2.310 Inf   5.709 <0.0001
##  3            -4.605 1.990 Inf  -2.313  0.0311
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(mod_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "sal_fpn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    1.664 2.40 Inf   0.694  0.7257
##  clust1 - clust3    2.683 2.27 Inf   1.181  0.7123
##  clust2 - clust3    1.019 2.91 Inf   0.351  0.7257
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -2.767 2.18 Inf  -1.270  0.3084
##  clust1 - clust3    0.699 2.19 Inf   0.319  0.7498
##  clust2 - clust3    3.466 2.74 Inf   1.266  0.3084
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    0.105 1.78 Inf   0.059  0.9531
##  clust1 - clust3    4.210 1.72 Inf   2.448  0.0431
##  clust2 - clust3    4.106 2.20 Inf   1.867  0.0929
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -8.168 1.79 Inf  -4.573 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3    2.461 1.66 Inf   1.481  0.1386
##  clust2 - clust3   10.629 2.16 Inf   4.914 <0.0001
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -1.454 2.32 Inf  -0.627  0.5304
##  clust1 - clust3    5.738 2.28 Inf   2.519  0.0199
##  clust2 - clust3    7.192 2.90 Inf   2.476  0.0199
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -13.569 2.57 Inf  -5.278 <0.0001
##  clust1 - clust3    4.223 2.29 Inf   1.845  0.0651
##  clust2 - clust3   17.792 3.05 Inf   5.835 <0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# supplementary findings (solely age/sex dependent)
mod_dmn_int <- lmer(
    bpm_int ~ dmn * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)
anova(mod_dmn_int)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                        Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn                    4.1401  4.1401     1 10990.9  6.7774 0.0092441 ** 
## clust                  5.4924  2.7462     2 10692.1  4.4957 0.0111785 *  
## age_c                  2.2198  2.2198     1  9696.3  3.6339 0.0566448 .  
## sex                    9.8975  9.8975     1 10644.4 16.2027 5.731e-05 ***
## changeage              0.1553  0.1553     1  2933.2  0.2543 0.6141032    
## pub_z                  7.1120  7.1120     1  5996.6 11.6427 0.0006488 ***
## rsfmri_meanmotion      3.7213  3.7213     1 10868.8  6.0918 0.0135960 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer  1.7888  0.8944     2     9.8  1.4642 0.2777719    
## dmn:clust              0.1936  0.0968     2 10921.9  0.1585 0.8534406    
## dmn:age_c              4.5352  4.5352     1  9651.5  7.4243 0.0064464 ** 
## clust:age_c            8.8505  4.4253     2  9689.1  7.2443 0.0007181 ***
## dmn:sex                5.3367  5.3367     1 10910.7  8.7364 0.0031258 ** 
## clust:sex              6.9910  3.4955     2 10724.6  5.7223 0.0032823 ** 
## age_c:sex             13.6433 13.6433     1  9676.6 22.3346 2.323e-06 ***
## dmn:clust:age_c        7.7107  3.8554     2  9652.7  6.3114 0.0018230 ** 
## dmn:clust:sex          6.4825  3.2412     2 10926.2  5.3060 0.0049743 ** 
## dmn:age_c:sex          4.3364  4.3364     1  9649.6  7.0989 0.0077259 ** 
## clust:age_c:sex        3.3784  1.6892     2  9689.3  2.7653 0.0630083 .  
## dmn:clust:age_c:sex    3.5458  1.7729     2  9652.6  2.9023 0.0549460 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
test(emtrends(mod_dmn_int, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "dmn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -1.447 0.821 Inf  -1.762  0.1170
##  2         0.678 1.540 Inf   0.439  0.6606
##  3        -4.448 1.510 Inf  -2.950  0.0095
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -0.686 0.781 Inf  -0.879  0.3792
##  2        -2.509 1.480 Inf  -1.697  0.1346
##  3        -3.864 1.390 Inf  -2.782  0.0162
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -1.281 0.623 Inf  -2.055  0.1197
##  2         1.501 1.190 Inf   1.263  0.3099
##  3         0.112 1.190 Inf   0.094  0.9249
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -0.635 0.621 Inf  -1.023  0.3062
##  2        -3.344 1.190 Inf  -2.799  0.0087
##  3        -3.016 1.090 Inf  -2.757  0.0087
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -1.114 0.807 Inf  -1.380  0.1675
##  2         2.324 1.510 Inf   1.535  0.1675
##  3         4.672 1.590 Inf   2.944  0.0097
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  clust dmn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        -0.584 0.850 Inf  -0.687  0.4918
##  2        -4.178 1.640 Inf  -2.549  0.0324
##  3        -2.168 1.410 Inf  -1.536  0.1868
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(mod_dmn_int, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "dmn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -2.125 1.74 Inf  -1.221  0.2221
##  clust1 - clust3    3.000 1.71 Inf   1.757  0.1184
##  clust2 - clust3    5.126 2.15 Inf   2.381  0.0518
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    1.823 1.66 Inf   1.095  0.4101
##  clust1 - clust3    3.178 1.59 Inf   2.000  0.1365
##  clust2 - clust3    1.355 2.02 Inf   0.670  0.5028
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -2.782 1.33 Inf  -2.088  0.1103
##  clust1 - clust3   -1.393 1.33 Inf  -1.046  0.4063
##  clust2 - clust3    1.389 1.67 Inf   0.830  0.4063
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    2.708 1.34 Inf   2.023  0.0860
##  clust1 - clust3    2.381 1.25 Inf   1.901  0.0860
##  clust2 - clust3   -0.328 1.61 Inf  -0.203  0.8392
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -3.438 1.71 Inf  -2.012  0.0664
##  clust1 - clust3   -5.786 1.77 Inf  -3.260  0.0033
##  clust2 - clust3   -2.348 2.19 Inf  -1.073  0.2832
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    3.594 1.84 Inf   1.950  0.1534
##  clust1 - clust3    1.583 1.64 Inf   0.963  0.3519
##  clust2 - clust3   -2.011 2.16 Inf  -0.931  0.3519
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
mod_fpn_ext <- lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ fpn * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)
anova(mod_fpn_ext)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                        Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## fpn                    1.0219  1.0219     1 11021.2  2.1750 0.1402993    
## clust                  6.3725  3.1862     2 10779.1  6.7813 0.0011396 ** 
## age_c                  1.2537  1.2537     1  9113.4  2.6684 0.1023957    
## sex                    3.3903  3.3903     1 10728.6  7.2157 0.0072380 ** 
## changeage              0.3417  0.3417     1  3337.9  0.7272 0.3938548    
## pub_z                  2.6028  2.6028     1  6134.3  5.5396 0.0186227 *  
## rsfmri_meanmotion      5.7090  5.7090     1 10788.7 12.1505 0.0004927 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer  1.2619  0.6309     2    12.0  1.3428 0.2975927    
## fpn:clust              0.4114  0.2057     2 10998.9  0.4378 0.6454368    
## fpn:age_c              0.2123  0.2123     1  9077.7  0.4518 0.5015059    
## clust:age_c            7.5323  3.7661     2  9122.1  8.0155 0.0003326 ***
## fpn:sex                0.8353  0.8353     1 10994.2  1.7778 0.1824423    
## clust:sex              1.6247  0.8123     2 10786.2  1.7289 0.1775274    
## age_c:sex             11.8358 11.8358     1  9114.9 25.1904 5.292e-07 ***
## fpn:clust:age_c        8.3005  4.1503     2  9087.9  8.8331 0.0001471 ***
## fpn:clust:sex          1.4514  0.7257     2 10992.9  1.5445 0.2134580    
## fpn:age_c:sex          7.7730  7.7730     1  9086.1 16.5434 4.795e-05 ***
## clust:age_c:sex       11.6905  5.8453     2  9128.6 12.4406 4.022e-06 ***
## fpn:clust:age_c:sex   10.8342  5.4171     2  9093.2 11.5293 9.982e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
test(emtrends(mod_fpn_ext, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "fpn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.

## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.

## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(mod_fpn_ext, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "fpn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")

mod_sal_fpn_ext <- lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ sal_fpn * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)
anova(mod_sal_fpn_ext)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                         Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)

## mri_info_manufacturer    1.332   0.666     2    11.4  1.4160 0.2823921

test(emtrends(mod_sal_fpn_ext, ~ clust | sex * age_c, var = "sal_fpn", at = list(age_c=c(-1,0,1))), adjust = "fdr")

mod_dmn_fpn_ext <- lmer(
    bpm_ext ~ dmn_fpn * clust * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)
anova(mod_dmn_fpn_ext)

## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11102' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11102)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -1.190 2.15 Inf  -0.554  0.5797
##  clust1 - clust3    5.533 2.10 Inf   2.632  0.0176
##  clust2 - clust3    6.723 2.67 Inf   2.519  0.0176
## 
## sex = 2, age_c = -1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    2.967 2.08 Inf   1.426  0.2639
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.452 1.94 Inf  -0.232  0.8163
##  clust2 - clust3   -3.419 2.53 Inf  -1.353  0.2639
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -1.711 1.61 Inf  -1.064  0.6813
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.663 1.61 Inf  -0.411  0.6813
##  clust2 - clust3    1.048 2.04 Inf   0.515  0.6813
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  0:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    2.970 1.58 Inf   1.875  0.1823
##  clust1 - clust3    0.332 1.53 Inf   0.217  0.8280
##  clust2 - clust3   -2.637 1.95 Inf  -1.355  0.2630
## 
## sex = 1, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -2.233 2.13 Inf  -1.049  0.2942
##  clust1 - clust3   -6.859 2.14 Inf  -3.211  0.0040
##  clust2 - clust3   -4.626 2.72 Inf  -1.701  0.1333
## 
## sex = 2, age_c =  1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    2.972 2.01 Inf   1.476  0.4197
##  clust1 - clust3    1.116 2.04 Inf   0.547  0.5842
##  clust2 - clust3   -1.856 2.50 Inf  -0.742  0.5842
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Plot cluster differences

custom_colors2 = c("#E9C46A", "#36827F", "#464D77")

# plot main interaction effect
mod_sal_fpn_2wayint <- lmer(
    bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_c + sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)

ggpredict(
    mod_sal_fpn_2wayint,
    terms = c("sal_fpn [all]", "clust")
) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    mutate(
        clust = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                       labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High disadvantage"))
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
        x = "SN-FPN Functional Connectivity",
        y = "Internalizing Symptoms",
        color = "Group", fill = "Group"
    ) + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2)

# plot
age_vals <- c(-1, 1)

ggpredict(
  mod_sal_fpn_int,
  terms = c(
    "sal_fpn [all]",                                  # x
    paste0("age_c [", paste(age_vals, collapse=","), "]"),  # group (linetype)
    "sex",                                            # facet (rows)
    "clust"                                           # panel (cols)
  )
) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(
    age_bin = factor(group,
      levels = as.character(age_vals),
      labels = c("Younger (-1 SD)", "Older (+1 SD)")
    ),
    sex = factor(facet, levels = c("1","2"), labels = c("Male","Female")),
    clust = factor(panel, levels = c("1","2","3"),
      labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High\ndisadvantage")
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust,
             linetype = age_bin, group = interaction(clust, age_bin))) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(sex ~ clust, labeller = labeller(sex = label_value, clust = label_value)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "SN-FPN Functional Connectivity", y = "Internalizing Symptoms",
       color = "Group", fill = "Group", linetype = "Age") +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Younger (-1 SD)"="dashed", "Older (+1 SD)"="solid"))

# simple pooled plot: sal_fpn (x) -> predicted symptoms (y)
# holds age_c at 0 (mean, since centered) and sex at its reference level;
# you can change those if you want (see note below)
m_pool = lmer(
    bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * age_c * sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude
)

pdat <- ggpredict(m_pool, terms = "sal_fpn [all]")

ggplot(pdat, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "SN-FPN Functional Connectivity",
    y = "Internalizing Symptoms"
  )

# plot the other models
specs <- list(
  dmnxint = list(mod = mod_dmn_int, x = "dmn", xlab = "DMN Functional Connectivity", ylab = "Internalizing Symptoms"),
  fpnxext    = list(mod = mod_fpn_ext,     x = "fpn",     xlab = "FPN Functional Connectivity",     ylab = "Externalizing Symptoms"),
  salfpnxext = list(mod = mod_sal_fpn_ext, x = "sal_fpn", xlab = "SN-FPN Functional Connectivity",  ylab = "Externalizing Symptoms"),
  dmnfpnxext = list(mod = mod_dmn_fpn_ext, x = "dmn_fpn", xlab = "DMN-FPN Functional Connectivity", ylab = "Externalizing Symptoms")
)

plots_supp <- lapply(specs, \(s)
  ggpredict(
    s$mod,
    terms = c(
      paste0(s$x, " [all]"),
      paste0("age_c [", paste(age_vals, collapse=","), "]"),
      "sex",
      "clust"
    )
  ) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    mutate(
      age_bin = factor(group, levels = as.character(age_vals),
                       labels = c("Younger (-1 SD)", "Older (+1 SD)")),
      sex = factor(facet, levels = c("1","2"), labels = c("Male","Female")),
      clust = factor(panel, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                     labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High\ndisadvantage"))
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust,
               linetype = age_bin, group = interaction(clust, age_bin))) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
    facet_grid(sex ~ clust, labeller = labeller(sex = label_value, clust = label_value)) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(x = s$xlab, y = s$ylab, color = "Group", fill = "Group", linetype = "Age") +
    scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Younger (-1 SD)"="dashed", "Older (+1 SD)"="solid"))
)

# view them
ggarrange(plots_supp$dmnxint, plots_supp$fpnxext, 
          plots_supp$salfpnxext, plots_supp$dmnfpnxext, common.legend=T, ncol=2, nrow=2)

Change on change effects

# outcomes (change scores)
agnes_res_env$d_bpm_int <- with(
  agnes_res_env,
  bpm_y_scr_internal_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 - bpm_y_scr_internal_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1
)
agnes_res_env$d_bpm_ext <- with(
  agnes_res_env,
  bpm_y_scr_external_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 - bpm_y_scr_external_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1
)

# brain change scores (edit this list if your wide columns use different names)
brains_base <- c("dmn_dmn","sal_sal","fpn_fpn","dmn_sal","dmn_fpn","sal_fpn")
for (b in brains_base) {
  agnes_res_env[[paste0("d_", b)]] <-
    agnes_res_env[[paste0(b, "_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1")]] -
    agnes_res_env[[paste0(b, "_baseline_year_1_arm_1")]]
}
brains <- paste0("d_", brains_base)

# FDR "families" (NO age_c in wide model)
families <- list(
  brain_clust     = ":clust",
  brain_clust_sex = ":clust:sex"
)

# change-score outcomes
outcomes <- c("d_bpm_int", "d_bpm_ext")

# fit models: outcome -> brain -> model
mods <- lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  m <- lapply(brains, \(b)
    lmer(
      as.formula(paste0(
        y, " ~ ", b, " * clust * sex + ",
        "age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z + mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr + ",
        "rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 + ",
        "(1 | site_id_l)"
      )),
      data = agnes_res_env, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights
    )
  )
  names(m) <- brains
  m
})
names(mods) <- outcomes

anv <- lapply(mods, \(mm) lapply(mm, anova))

# within-outcome FDR across 6 brains, per family
p_all_coc <- bind_rows(lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  bind_rows(lapply(names(families), \(fam) {
    suf <- families[[fam]]
    pvec <- sapply(brains, \(b) anv[[y]][[b]][paste0(b, suf), "Pr(>F)"])
    tibble(outcome = y, family = fam, brain = brains, p = as.numeric(pvec),
           p_fdr = p.adjust(pvec, "fdr"))
  })) 
})) 

# quick views
p_all_coc %>% 
  filter(brain=="d_sal_fpn" & outcome == "d_bpm_int") %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3)))
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   outcome   family          brain         p p_fdr
##   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>     <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 d_bpm_int brain_clust     d_sal_fpn 0.005 0.032
## 2 d_bpm_int brain_clust_sex d_sal_fpn 0.004 0.023
p_all_coc %>% 
  filter(brain=="d_sal_sal" & outcome == "d_bpm_ext") %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3)))
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   outcome   family          brain         p p_fdr
##   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>     <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 d_bpm_ext brain_clust     d_sal_sal 0.699 0.857
## 2 d_bpm_ext brain_clust_sex d_sal_sal 0.003 0.02
p_all_coc %>% 
  filter(brain=="d_dmn_fpn" & outcome == "d_bpm_ext") %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3)))
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   outcome   family          brain         p p_fdr
##   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>     <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 d_bpm_ext brain_clust     d_dmn_fpn 0.131 0.361
## 2 d_bpm_ext brain_clust_sex d_dmn_fpn 0.007 0.02
anv <- lapply(mods, \(mm) lapply(mm, anova))

p_all_coc <- bind_rows(lapply(outcomes, \(y) {
  bind_rows(lapply(brains, \(b) {
    a <- as.data.frame(anv[[y]][[b]])
    tibble(
      outcome = y,
      brain   = b,
      term    = rownames(a),
      p       = a[["Pr(>F)"]],
      term_std = vapply(strsplit(rownames(a), ":"), \(x) {
        paste(replace(x, x == b, "brain"), collapse = ":")
      }, character(1))
    )
  }))
})) %>%
  group_by(outcome, term_std) %>%
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()

# all corrected p-values (no filtering)
p_all_coc %>% arrange(outcome, term_std, brain)
## # A tibble: 156 × 6
##    outcome   brain     term                              p term_std        p_fdr
##    <chr>     <chr>     <chr>                         <dbl> <chr>           <dbl>
##  1 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_dmn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000718 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  2 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000114  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  3 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_sal age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000841 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  4 d_bpm_ext d_fpn_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000731 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  5 d_bpm_ext d_sal_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000116  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  6 d_bpm_ext d_sal_sal age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000117  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  7 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_dmn d_dmn_dmn                 0.605     brain         7.85e-1
##  8 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_fpn d_dmn_fpn                 0.675     brain         7.85e-1
##  9 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_sal d_dmn_sal                 0.785     brain         7.85e-1
## 10 d_bpm_ext d_fpn_fpn d_fpn_fpn                 0.276     brain         5.52e-1
## # ℹ 146 more rows
# significant only (optional)
p_all_coc %>% filter(p_fdr < .05) %>% arrange(outcome, term_std, p_fdr, brain)
## # A tibble: 35 × 6
##    outcome   brain     term                              p term_std        p_fdr
##    <chr>     <chr>     <chr>                         <dbl> <chr>           <dbl>
##  1 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_dmn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000718 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  2 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000114  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  3 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_sal age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000841 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  4 d_bpm_ext d_fpn_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.0000731 age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  5 d_bpm_ext d_sal_fpn age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000116  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  6 d_bpm_ext d_sal_sal age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.000117  age_baseline… 1.17e-4
##  7 d_bpm_ext d_sal_sal d_sal_sal:clust:sex       0.00339   brain:clust:… 2.03e-2
##  8 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_fpn d_dmn_fpn:clust:sex       0.00682   brain:clust:… 2.05e-2
##  9 d_bpm_ext d_dmn_dmn sex                       0.00981   sex           1.53e-2
## 10 d_bpm_ext d_fpn_fpn sex                       0.0102    sex           1.53e-2
## # ℹ 25 more rows

Probe significant change on change findings

# change on change effects - main finding
coc_sal_fpn_int <- lmer(d_bpm_int ~
    d_sal_fpn * clust * sex + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z + mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr +
    rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 +
    (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env, weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude)
anova(coc_sal_fpn_int)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value
## d_sal_fpn                                   0.021   0.021     1 4153.3  0.0136
## clust                                       8.138   4.069     2 4120.1  2.6467
## sex                                        70.729  70.729     1 4145.6 46.0080
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  31.908  31.908     1 4065.9 20.7556
## pub_z                                       4.089   4.089     1 4154.3  2.6596
## mri_info_manufacturer                       1.009   0.504     2   53.9  0.3282
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                    1.070   1.070     1 3256.5  0.6957
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1     0.522   0.522     1 4150.9  0.3397
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1  2.601   2.601     1 4121.6  1.6918
## d_sal_fpn:clust                            16.157   8.079     2 4148.5  5.2550
## d_sal_fpn:sex                               5.430   5.430     1 4139.7  3.5318
## clust:sex                                  17.918   8.959     2 4143.5  5.8278
## d_sal_fpn:clust:sex                        17.089   8.544     2 4143.5  5.5581
##                                               Pr(>F)    
## d_sal_fpn                                   0.907231    
## clust                                       0.071004 .  
## sex                                        1.344e-11 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  5.371e-06 ***
## pub_z                                       0.103002    
## mri_info_manufacturer                       0.721684    
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                    0.404287    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1     0.560043    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1  0.193433    
## d_sal_fpn:clust                             0.005256 ** 
## d_sal_fpn:sex                               0.060271 .  
## clust:sex                                   0.002969 ** 
## d_sal_fpn:clust:sex                         0.003885 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
pairs(emtrends(coc_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust, var = "d_sal_fpn"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -1.57 2.08 Inf  -0.758  0.4487
##  clust1 - clust3     5.79 1.98 Inf   2.915  0.0057
##  clust2 - clust3     7.36 2.54 Inf   2.892  0.0057
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex, mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(coc_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_sal_fpn"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  clust d_sal_fpn.trend   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1              3.1201 1.34 Inf   2.335  0.0293
##  2             -1.9042 2.66 Inf  -0.716  0.4738
##  3             -5.9872 2.48 Inf  -2.416  0.0293
## 
## sex = 2:
##  clust d_sal_fpn.trend   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             -0.0994 1.33 Inf  -0.075  0.9405
##  2              8.0692 2.57 Inf   3.136  0.0051
##  3             -2.5626 2.46 Inf  -1.041  0.4470
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(coc_sal_fpn_int, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_sal_fpn"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4176' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4176)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     5.02 2.97 Inf   1.689  0.1368
##  clust1 - clust3     9.11 2.81 Inf   3.237  0.0036
##  clust2 - clust3     4.08 3.63 Inf   1.125  0.2606
## 
## sex = 2:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -8.17 2.90 Inf  -2.821  0.0072
##  clust1 - clust3     2.46 2.80 Inf   0.881  0.3783
##  clust2 - clust3    10.63 3.56 Inf   2.987  0.0072
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# change on change effects - supplementary finding
coc_sal_ext <- lmer(
  d_bpm_ext ~
    d_sal_sal * clust * sex + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z + mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr +
    rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 +
    (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env, weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude)
anova(coc_sal_ext)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value
## d_sal_sal                                   2.4970  2.4970     1 4094.9  2.1116
## clust                                       4.5748  2.2874     2 3982.4  1.9343
## sex                                         8.6227  8.6227     1 4092.0  7.2917
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  17.5879 17.5879     1 3819.1 14.8731
## pub_z                                       2.4537  2.4537     1 4043.4  2.0750
## mri_info_manufacturer                       0.9362  0.4681     2   39.7  0.3959
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                    1.3954  1.3954     1 2490.7  1.1800
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1     0.4489  0.4489     1 4070.7  0.3796
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1  0.5452  0.5452     1 4013.8  0.4611
## d_sal_sal:clust                             0.8467  0.4233     2 4091.0  0.3580
## d_sal_sal:sex                               1.0590  1.0590     1 4089.8  0.8955
## clust:sex                                   1.8759  0.9380     2 4089.5  0.7932
## d_sal_sal:clust:sex                        13.4695  6.7348     2 4093.6  5.6952
##                                               Pr(>F)    
## d_sal_sal                                  0.1462651    
## clust                                      0.1446553    
## sex                                        0.0069557 ** 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  0.0001169 ***
## pub_z                                      0.1498077    
## mri_info_manufacturer                      0.6757332    
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                   0.2774602    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1    0.5378676    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 0.4971599    
## d_sal_sal:clust                            0.6991021    
## d_sal_sal:sex                              0.3440438    
## clust:sex                                  0.4524735    
## d_sal_sal:clust:sex                        0.0033888 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
test(emtrends(coc_sal_ext, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_sal_sal"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4114' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4114)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4114' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4114)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  clust d_sal_sal.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1              -0.149 0.829 Inf  -0.180  0.9063
##  2              -3.880 1.560 Inf  -2.485  0.0389
##  3               0.177 1.500 Inf   0.118  0.9063
## 
## sex = 2:
##  clust d_sal_sal.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1              -0.553 0.850 Inf  -0.651  0.5151
##  2               2.602 1.470 Inf   1.774  0.1142
##  3              -2.883 1.400 Inf  -2.056  0.1142
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(coc_sal_ext, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_sal_sal"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4114' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4114)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4114' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4114)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    3.731 1.77 Inf   2.111  0.0912
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.326 1.71 Inf  -0.190  0.8490
##  clust2 - clust3   -4.057 2.16 Inf  -1.875  0.0912
## 
## sex = 2:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -3.156 1.69 Inf  -1.865  0.0933
##  clust1 - clust3    2.329 1.64 Inf   1.423  0.1548
##  clust2 - clust3    5.485 2.02 Inf   2.710  0.0202
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
coc_dmn_fpn_ext <- lmer(d_bpm_ext ~
    d_dmn_fpn * clust * sex + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z + mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr +
    rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 +
    (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env, weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude)
anova(coc_dmn_fpn_ext)
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value
## d_dmn_fpn                                   0.2079  0.2079     1 4096.9  0.1756
## clust                                       4.3358  2.1679     2 3990.6  1.8318
## sex                                         6.6568  6.6568     1 4093.3  5.6247
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  17.6489 17.6489     1 3818.8 14.9125
## pub_z                                       2.1942  2.1942     1 4048.3  1.8540
## mri_info_manufacturer                       0.9559  0.4780     2   40.2  0.4039
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                    1.3616  1.3616     1 2481.0  1.1505
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1     0.1533  0.1533     1 4066.8  0.1295
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1  0.6721  0.6721     1 4021.3  0.5679
## d_dmn_fpn:clust                             4.8100  2.4050     2 4094.2  2.0321
## d_dmn_fpn:sex                               7.8906  7.8906     1 4090.3  6.6672
## clust:sex                                   0.9495  0.4748     2 4091.6  0.4012
## d_dmn_fpn:clust:sex                        11.8197  5.9099     2 4092.2  4.9936
##                                               Pr(>F)    
## d_dmn_fpn                                  0.6751619    
## clust                                      0.1602613    
## sex                                        0.0177554 *  
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                  0.0001145 ***
## pub_z                                      0.1733939    
## mri_info_manufacturer                      0.6704216    
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                   0.2835581    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1    0.7189630    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 0.4511392    
## d_dmn_fpn:clust                            0.1311880    
## d_dmn_fpn:sex                              0.0098549 ** 
## clust:sex                                  0.6695711    
## d_dmn_fpn:clust:sex                        0.0068229 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
test(emtrends(coc_dmn_fpn_ext, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_dmn_fpn"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4116' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4116)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4116' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4116)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  clust d_dmn_fpn.trend   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1              -0.501 1.20 Inf  -0.418  0.6759
##  2               2.635 2.34 Inf   1.127  0.3897
##  3               3.108 2.28 Inf   1.363  0.3897
## 
## sex = 2:
##  clust d_dmn_fpn.trend   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1               0.369 1.22 Inf   0.303  0.8176
##  2              -8.156 2.32 Inf  -3.516  0.0013
##  3               0.498 2.16 Inf   0.231  0.8176
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
pairs(emtrends(coc_dmn_fpn_ext, ~ clust | sex, var = "d_dmn_fpn"), adjust = "fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 4116' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 4116)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 4116' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 4116)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## sex = 1:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -3.136 2.63 Inf  -1.194  0.3484
##  clust1 - clust3   -3.609 2.57 Inf  -1.402  0.3484
##  clust2 - clust3   -0.473 3.27 Inf  -0.145  0.8849
## 
## sex = 2:
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    8.525 2.62 Inf   3.259  0.0034
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.129 2.48 Inf  -0.052  0.9584
##  clust2 - clust3   -8.655 3.17 Inf  -2.729  0.0095
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Plot change on change

### plot change on change - main finding
ggpredict(
  coc_sal_fpn_int,
  terms = c("d_sal_fpn [all]", "clust", "sex")
) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(
    clust = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                   labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High disadvantage")),
    sex = factor(facet, levels = c("1","2"), labels = c("Male","Female"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(sex ~ clust, labeller = labeller(sex = label_value, clust = label_value)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Δ SN-FPN Functional Connectivity",
    y = "Δ Internalizing Symptoms",
    color = "Group", fill = "Group"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2)

### plot change on change - supplementary finding
ggarrange(
  (ggpredict(
  coc_sal_ext,
  terms = c("d_sal_sal [all]", "clust", "sex")
) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(
    clust = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                   labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High\ndisadvantage")),
    sex = factor(facet, levels = c("1","2"), labels = c("Male","Female"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(sex ~ clust, labeller = labeller(sex = label_value, clust = label_value)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Δ SN-SN Functional Connectivity",
    y = "Δ Externalizing Symptoms",
    color = "Group", fill = "Group"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2)), 
(ggpredict(
  coc_dmn_fpn_ext,
  terms = c("d_dmn_fpn [all]", "clust", "sex")
) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(
    clust = factor(group, levels = c("1","2","3"),
                   labels = c("Low risk", "High trauma\n& familial risk", "High\ndisadvantage")),
    sex = factor(facet, levels = c("1","2"), labels = c("Male","Female"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = clust, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.10, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(sex ~ clust, labeller = labeller(sex = label_value, clust = label_value)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Δ DMN-FPN Functional Connectivity",
    y = "Δ Externalizing Symptoms",
    color = "Group", fill = "Group"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors2) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors2)), 
common.legend=T, ncol=2)

Pooled analysis

summary(lmer(
    bpm_int ~ sal_fpn + age_c + sex + changeage + 
        pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion + mri_info_manufacturer +
        (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
    data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c)),
    weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: 
## bpm_int ~ sal_fpn + age_c + sex + changeage + pub_z + rsfmri_meanmotion +  
##     mri_info_manufacturer + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
##    Data: agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(age_c))
## Weights: weights
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: Inf
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.5303 -0.4660 -0.1941  0.3138  7.1578 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups         Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  src_subject_id (Intercept) 2.15405  1.4677  
##  site_id_l      (Intercept) 0.02849  0.1688  
##  Residual                   0.62440  0.7902  
## Number of obs: 11151, groups:  src_subject_id, 6975; site_id_l, 22
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                Estimate Std. Error         df
## (Intercept)                                   1.662e+00  2.554e-01  5.129e+02
## sal_fpn                                       6.722e-01  4.625e-01  1.108e+04
## age_c                                         3.980e-02  1.764e-02  9.333e+03
## sex2                                          1.171e-01  5.472e-02  6.293e+03
## changeage                                     3.882e-02  1.163e-01  3.802e+03
## pub_z                                         1.497e-01  2.738e-02  6.031e+03
## rsfmri_meanmotion                             3.252e-01  1.063e-01  1.092e+04
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems -3.606e-01  1.522e-01  1.588e+01
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                 -1.784e-01  1.058e-01  1.450e+01
##                                              t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                    6.508 1.81e-10 ***
## sal_fpn                                        1.453  0.14615    
## age_c                                          2.256  0.02407 *  
## sex2                                           2.139  0.03247 *  
## changeage                                      0.334  0.73850    
## pub_z                                          5.468 4.72e-08 ***
## rsfmri_meanmotion                              3.061  0.00221 ** 
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems  -2.369  0.03089 *  
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                  -1.686  0.11321    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) sl_fpn age_c  sex2   changg pub_z  rsfmr_ m__PMS
## sal_fpn      0.038                                                 
## age_c       -0.057  0.020                                          
## sex2        -0.102  0.040  0.096                                   
## changeage   -0.927 -0.019  0.042 -0.010                            
## pub_z        0.052  0.006 -0.100 -0.483 -0.001                     
## rsfmr_mnmtn -0.069 -0.083  0.238  0.081 -0.020 -0.029              
## mr_nf_mnPMS -0.174 -0.033 -0.013 -0.004 -0.033  0.009 -0.015       
## mr__SIEMENS -0.241 -0.022 -0.016  0.013 -0.058 -0.015 -0.013  0.498
summary(lmer(d_bpm_int ~
                            d_sal_fpn + sex + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z + mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr +
                            rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 +
                            (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env, weights = weights, na.action = na.exclude))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: d_bpm_int ~ d_sal_fpn + sex + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + pub_z +  
##     mri_info_manufacturer + changeage_frombase_to2yr + rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1 +  
##     rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l)
##    Data: agnes_res_env
## Weights: weights
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: Inf
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -6.9146 -0.4226 -0.0065  0.3507  7.4891 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups    Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  site_id_l (Intercept) 0.02993  0.173   
##  Residual              1.54791  1.244   
## Number of obs: 4176, groups:  site_id_l, 22
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                Estimate Std. Error         df
## (Intercept)                                  -3.046e+00  7.425e-01  3.395e+03
## d_sal_fpn                                     7.761e-01  7.636e-01  4.162e+03
## sex2                                          5.789e-01  8.204e-02  4.157e+03
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                     2.604e-01  5.938e-02  4.065e+03
## pub_z                                         7.240e-02  4.073e-02  4.157e+03
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems -1.461e-01  1.882e-01  5.623e+01
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                  5.114e-03  1.266e-01  4.200e+01
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                      1.401e-01  1.995e-01  3.235e+03
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1      -1.179e-01  1.836e-01  4.158e+03
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1    3.526e-01  2.853e-01  4.127e+03
##                                              t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                   -4.102 4.19e-05 ***
## d_sal_fpn                                      1.016   0.3095    
## sex2                                           7.056 2.00e-12 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                      4.385 1.19e-05 ***
## pub_z                                          1.778   0.0755 .  
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems  -0.776   0.4408    
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                   0.040   0.9680    
## changeage_frombase_to2yr                       0.703   0.4824    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1       -0.642   0.5209    
## rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1     1.236   0.2167    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) d_sl_f sex2   a___1_ pub_z  m__PMS m__SIE chn__2 r____1
## d_sal_fpn    0.006                                                        
## sex2        -0.224  0.039                                                 
## ag_bs__1__1 -0.828  0.000  0.196                                          
## pub_z        0.225  0.007 -0.506 -0.254                                   
## mr_nf_mnPMS -0.030  0.034 -0.013 -0.022  0.026                            
## mr__SIEMENS -0.056  0.065 -0.005 -0.027  0.000  0.485                     
## chngg_frm_2 -0.554 -0.027  0.014  0.022  0.008 -0.060 -0.079              
## rsf____1__1 -0.092  0.038  0.052  0.088 -0.044 -0.006 -0.013 -0.029       
## r__2______1 -0.127 -0.022  0.090  0.091  0.005 -0.023 -0.016  0.015 -0.311
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## Gradient contains NAs
